response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and D programming, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. There is no demonstrated expertise in Java or Kotlin beyond a surface-level skills mention, no experience with Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate, and no evidence of JVM internals knowledge. Her only IDE-adjacent experience is writing C components for NI LabWindows/CVI — a C IDE — which is architecturally unrelated to JVM-based IDE development. The role demands deep JVM tooling and enterprise Java ecosystem ownership that is simply not present in this resume.
strengths: Genuine interest in developer tooling demonstrated through NI LabWindows/CVI work and open-source test framework projects (Trial, Fluent-Asserts for D lang)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and consistent community engagement via talks and workshops
Experience building and scaling high-throughput distributed systems (Sociomantic micro-services at millions of HTTP requests per second)
concerns: No Java or Kotlin expertise at the required depth — Java appears only in passing (robotic arm work at Altom) and Kotlin is entirely absent
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-based enterprise framework, which are explicit core requirements
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js/D-lang — a fundamentally different ecosystem from JVM tooling and IDE internals
